content.problemStatement:
You are given two positive integers XXX and YYY.

Consider an array AAA consisting of XXX occurrences of 111 and YYY occurrences of −2-2−2.

You can rearrange the elements of AAA in any order.

Rearrange the elements to **minimize** the maximum sum over all subarrays of AAA.

Find the maximum subarray sum of such rearrangement of AAA.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY, denoting the number of occurrences of 111 and −2-2−2 respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, print the maximum sum of subarray if you rearrange AAA optimally.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the number of occurrences of 1 and -2 respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer — the minimum possible value of the maximum subarray sum after rearranging the array optimally.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we compute ceil(X/(Y+1)).
1) ceil(1/2)=1
2) ceil(1/4)=1
3) ceil(5/2)=3
4) ceil(2/3)=1

solution.approach: The maximum subarray sum of any arrangement will always be the sum of a longest contiguous block of 1's, because adding a -2 can only decrease the sum of a segment. To minimise this value we must minimise the length of the longest block of 1's. With Y occurrences of -2 we can place them as separators, creating at most Y+1 blocks of 1's. Distributing X ones as evenly as possible among these blocks gives the smallest possible maximum block size, which is ceil( X / (Y+1) ). The answer can be computed with integer arithmetic as (X + Y) // (Y + 1). The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        ans = (X + Y) // (Y + 1)  # ceil division
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
